behavior_summary: This script provides a set of read/inspect handlers for in-game scroll/letter items. Each named function spawns the engine UI process Scroll::read with a fixed string (flavor, clue, or quest text) and then yields (suspend) until the read process completes.
No functions perform branching, flag reads, or flag writes; their sole runtime effect is to present text to the player and wait for the read UI to finish.
Strings passed to Scroll::read are hard-coded and differ per handler (shopping list, dying notes, letters, potion descriptions, ritual instructions, etc.).
notable_control_flow: Each handler calls str_to_ptr(literal) and then spawns Scroll::read(textPtr, this) using the engine spawn idiom (pid <=> process ...).
The spawned process id is captured into the local 'temp' and the caller immediately issues suspend to yield until the read/display process completes.
There is no internal branching or interaction between handlers; handlers appear to be independent and likely bound separately to different scroll item IDs or read events.
open_questions: Which game item IDs or editor bindings call each handler (for example which item uses U8P_SCROLL1::func0080 vs U8P_SCROLL1::func027F)? (inferred but not present in this file)
Why are there near-duplicate handlers (e.g., U8P_SCROLL1::func0872 and U8P_SCROLL1::func0AB8) — are these separate item variants, localization differences, or duplicate entries? (uncertain)
The canonical process_type is 0x0001 for these functions; are these executed as engine processes or direct function handlers in the run-time wiring? (inferred as read handlers; confidence medium)

main_function.name: U8P_SCROLL1::func0080
main_function.purpose: Present a short shopping-list-style scroll text to the player by spawning Scroll::read with the literal string and yielding until the read completes.
entry_conditions: Player reads/uses or examines the associated scroll item in the game (handler invoked by engine/item binding).
Engine spawns this handler when the read action is requested for the item tied to this function.

function_map.name: Scroll::read
function_map.role: Engine-provided UI process that displays scroll/letter text to the player.

external_calls.purpose: Display the shopping-list scroll text to the player in a read UI; called with a pointer to the literal string and the invoking context ('this').

Spawn the Scroll::read process and return a pid which is captured into 'temp' (pid <=> process ... idiom).

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: This script provides a set of read/inspect handlers for in-game scroll/letter items. Each named function spawns the engine UI process Scroll::read with a fixed string (flavor, clue, or quest text) and then yields (suspend) until the read process completes.
